paper_title: Polytene Chromosome Analysis in Chironomus sp. prope agilis

methods.description: Preparation of polytene chromosome slides from VI instar larvae for chromosomal mapping and analysis.

steps.description: The VI instar larvae from 8 natural populations from Russia (the Urals and Siberia) and Kazakhstan were used for polytene chromosome slide preparation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect VI instar larvae from specified populations

steps.description: The larvae were fixed with 3:1 v/v of 96% ethanol and glacial acetic acid and stored at -20 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: fix larvae with ethanol and acetic acid, store at -20°C

steps.description: Polytene chromosome squashes were prepared by the routine aceto-orcein method.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare chromosome squashes using aceto-orcein method

methods.description: Mapping of chromosomal arms using established mapping systems to identify banding sequences.

steps.description: Chromosomal mapping of arms A, C, D, E, and F was done using the mapping system created by Keyl and Devai, with Ch. piger as the standard karyotype.
steps.searchable_summary: map chromosomal arms using Keyl and Devai systems

steps.description: Mapping of arm B was done according to the Maximova mapping system, improved by Schobanov.
steps.searchable_summary: map arm B using Maximova and Schobanov systems

methods.description: Statistical analysis of chromosomal data using the PHYLIP software package.

steps.description: Statistical analysis was done using the program PHYLIP.
